feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Azure /IAS User filtering
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m using Azure as IdP and IAS as a proxy.
In Azure, I have created groups which are then provisioned to cloud applications via IAS. In IPS I have set up a source system for Azure.

My requirement is to provision only specific groups and their users to IAS – for example, all groups whose names start with "SAP_" – without having to constantly modify the JSON transformation code.

To achieve this, I have configured the following parameters in the source system:

aad.group.filter = startsWith(displayName,'SAP_')

aad.user.filter.group.filter.combine = true

However, it seems that with these settings, all users in Azure are still being queried, which – as far as I understand – can lead to performance issues, especially in large environments.

My questions:

Is there a way to use the aad.user.filter parameter to ensure that only users who belong to the filtered groups (e.g., those with the prefix SAP_) are provisioned, without having to hardcode group names or regularly change the transformation code?

Is it possible to implement something like this:

aad.user.filter = users who belong to the groups defined in aad.group.filter

What is the recommended approach to efficiently and dynamically filter only the required groups and their members?

Thank you in advance for your support

 

Many Thanks

entry.title: SAP IAS - Can Application Custom Attributes Be Used in Provisioning to the Target System
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

We are currently setting up user provisioning between SAP IAS as the source system and SAP Ariba as the target system, using SAP Identity Provisioning Service.

My question is about the Application Custom Attributes, which can be found in IAS under:
Users & Authorizations > User Management > [Select a User] > Applications.

Can these Application Custom Attributes (1–5) be used in the target system transformation mapping during provisioning?

We would like to use the values of these Application Custom Attributes in our IPS transformation mapping to populate fields in the target system (Ariba).

If it's not possible to use the Application Custom Attributes during the Provisioning, what are they used for?

Any clarification or best practice from the community would be highly appreciated!

 

Best regards 

Maurice

entry.title: SAP system startup FMA
entry.content:
SAP Public Cloud Edition How to Enable MFA for Multiple Authentication at Login
For example, if I want to log in, I need a mobile phone verification code.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Single Sign On - all Hybrid SAP landscape - One Identity
entry.content:
Hello,

We are reviewing the possibilities of configuring one identity to enable single sign on all SAP ecosystems (cloud and onpremise systems). 
Has anyone configured it in the way that users logs in automatically to both SAP cloud systems as well as on premise systems without having to provide credentials again?

Do we have a procedure or guide to understand what and how this can be done using IAS and Azure Entra as our corporate ID.

Appretiate your inputs in this regards.

Thank you.

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: SAP IAS Federated Access AWS Cognito
entry.content:
Hello SAP-Community,

In a recent project for a client, we had a feature request that consisted of making a Fiori application available in SAP BTP Workzone, so far so good. Currently, this client has a mobile application developed by a third party that uses AWS Cognito as an identity provider (IdP) and our need is to provide access to this Fiori application without a request new login.

My question would be whether it is possible to establish a trust relationship between SAP IAS and AWS Cognito so that a new login operation is not required? If so, is there any documentation or a step-by-step guide to help us?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP CIS - User shows undefined when authentication to an application using corporate IDP
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We have an OIDC application created within SAP BTP and it has trust with SAP Cloud identity services. The application works as expected when we use Identity authentication as IDP. But when we switch the IDP to Corporate IDP (which is Entra ID), the authentication is successful however the app launches with no tiles and user shows as undefined. 

Identity federation (user store) is disabled and we have enriched the assertion attributes. Any thoughts on what could be the issue?

entry.title: SAP IPS connector is creating a different UserID in the SAP ECP environment
entry.content:
After running the IPS job, the user is created with a different userID than the one it has in the IAS environment.

Our goal is for the UserID to be the same in both environments.

We don't have custom transformations and logged error messages.

Statistics:

 
 EntitySystemActionReadCreatedUpdatedDeletedSkippedFailed   
 groupIDDS_DEVREAD100000  
 userIDDS_DEVREAD100000  
 groupSF_ECP_DEV_4_IDDS_2WRITE010000  
 userSF_ECP_DEV_4_IDDS_2WRITE010000

Statistics:

 
 EntitySystemActionReadCreatedUpdatedDeletedSkippedFailed   
 groupIDDS_DEVREAD100000  
 userIDDS_DEVREAD100000  
 groupSF_ECP_DEV_4_IDDS_2WRITE001000  
 userSF_ECP_DEV_4_IDDS_2WRITE001000

entry.title: Merging Users in IAS
entry.content:
Are there any tips/techniques to merge users/security groups from 2 different source systems in IAS?

The Source Systems each contain specific information regarding access for the user.

When a sync is triggered, all of the existing security groups are cleared and only the security group for the current source system being synched are added back. Ideally, the sync will only clear/add security groups tied to the system being synched.

entry.title: SAP BTP Cockpit Access using Corporate IDP for SAP Support
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are using our Corporate IDP to authenticate users to access BTP Cockpit via Cloud Identity Service. I know that we can add users to Cloud Identity Service and access services in BTP using the Cloud Identity Service user and password ( i.e. CLI Login, SAP BTP API etc)  but not for Cockpit Login since when you login to the cockpit it will invoke the MFA from our Corporate IDP.

I was wondering if there is a way where we can provide SAP with a user that can login to our BTP Cockpit without passing through Corporate IDP MFA and only using Cloud Identity Service credential?

 

Note: We are moving away from SAP IDS and I am looking for ways to provide SAP access when we have issues with our platform.

Thank you in Advance!

Alden

entry.title: Materials to learn SAP Cloud Indentity Services, IAS, XSUAA etc.
entry.content:
Hello,

are there any learning courses, videos, books or different materials to dive deep into Cloud Identity Service topics or general authentication/provisioning at SAP topics? So far I was only able to find an introduction: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/introducing-sap-cloud-identity-services.

Any resources are appreciated,
Best regards
Davit

entry.title: How to assign users to specific group in target system via transformation in IAS
entry.content:
While provisioning users to target system in Identity Authentication (IAS), there is a need to automatically assign users to specific group in target system. So, the source system for us is SuccessFactors and Target System is IAS also we are using Identity Authentication SCIM API version 1. Any of the user created in Source System should automatically get created in Target system. 

So the 1st condition any user with the mail id '@gmail.com' should get assigned into one group named as 'Workzone_user' in IAS .

2nd Condition Any user with userType as Employee should get assigned to same group ('Workzone_User') in IAS .

We followed the 2984603 - How to assign users to specific group in target system via transformation - SAP for Me this sap note &  SAP Help Portal this blog and tried writing the transformation code as follows:

{
  "condition": "$.userType =~ /.*Employee.*/",
  "constant": "Workzone_User",
  "targetPath": "$.groups[0].value"
},

{
  "condition": "$.emails[0].value =~ /.*@gmail.com/",
  "constant": "Workzone_End_User",
  "targetPath": "$.groups[1].value"
},

Following error message was showed up when we ran the job :-
"error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://anrc3jtv4.accounts.cloud.sap/service/scim/Users with statusCode: 401, Response:"

Solely if only 1st condition is also working that is also fine for that specific condition to work we wrote this code but after we ran the job still it was failing 

{
"condition": "$.emails[0].value =~ /.*@gmail.com/",
}

Following are the configuration we have done in target system properties: 

Please let us know where we are going wrong or what approach needs to be followed here for the transformation.

entry.title: Integration of Joule with IAS for SAP SuccessFactors and S4 HANA: Procedure and Impact
entry.content:
We have integrated Joule with IAS, which is linked to S4 HANA. Can we also link the same Joule service with the IAS of SAP SuccessFactors? Additionally, we have two IAS instances: one for SAP SuccessFactors and another for S4 HANA.

Could you please provide the procedure if this integration is possible? If not, what alternatives do we have?

What will be the impact on the systems and data in both scenarios?

entry.title: IPS - delete user
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I’m using IPS to provision users from IAS to SAP Enable Now.
When I remove a user from the IAS group, they are not deleted from Enable Now after the IPS job runs.

Is there a way to automatically delete users in Enable Now via IPS?

Many Thanks

 

Best regards

entry.title: IAS Logging Questions
entry.content:
I have a customer that want to be able to pull Authentication logs from IAS via API. Is this possible?

Also, what is the retention policy for the IAS logs?

 

Thanks in advance.

Phillip

entry.title: IPS Job finished with error: error=The request to create an entity has failed with: 409 Conflict 'A
entry.content:
Dear all, 

 

Dear all, I have the following use case: I ran an IPS job, and it finished with an error. The reason is that a user was already created before the job ran. Is there a solution to avoid this problem? I discovered that I need to delete the user: 2907578 - Failed or missing users during IPS provisioning - User profile with email [<name>@<domain>] already exists | SAP Knowledge Base Article

Can the IPS job recognize the user and simply update it?

Thanks for your support.

entry.title: Adjustment to the provisioning script (IPS Job)
entry.content: Hello, I have the following scenario. Currently, provisioning reads 20,000 users, which causes the job to run for almost an hour. Is there a way to only read members of a group, so that only the 20 users are read and provisioned directly instead of always reading 20,000 users?

entry.title: Express Node.js Multi-Tenant Backend Application with XSUAA on BTP - Non CAP based
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have created a Multi tenant SAAS application (Non-CAP NodeJS based) and have deployed this app to SAP BTP in the Provider subaccount. Along with the application I have the saas-registry instance and xsuaa instance created in my Provider subaccount. I am also able to subscribe to this application from the Consumer subaccount.


Current Status: So far, I have successfully implemented application subscription from tenant subaccounts using the saas-registry and an xsuaa instance. I’ve also implemented the necessary logic for subscribe and unsubscribe events in the backend.

Note: This is a pure backend application, and I am not using an Approuter, as there is no frontend component involved. The application is intended to be consumed by tenant-specific backend or frontend apps via API calls.

Problems:

  1. When I subscribe app in consumer subaccount, the xsuaa instance (using tenant-mode as "shared in provider subaccount) is not automatically created in consumer subaccount.
  2. To address this, I added event for getting dependencies while subscribing in backend the code is shown in below picture.
  3. Also added "url" for "getDependencies" in "appUrls" and dependencies detail in SaaS configuration file as shown below.
  4. After these changes, I tried subscribing to the app again from the consumer subaccount got an error shown below.

Asking for Suggestions:

  1. How can I achieve the automatic creation of an XSUAA instance in the consumer subaccount when subscribing to the app, to ensure full isolation between consumer subaccounts?
  2. If not then how can I achieve independent OAuth 2.0 Authentication for tenants specific to their subaccount?

entry.title: How do setup IAS login page for Onboardee and SSO page for Employee authentication?
entry.content:
The client has set up IAS and SSO for SuccessFactors. The expected behavior is that employees will log in via SSO, while onboardees and other users will use the IAS login process.

Current Configuration
The default authenticating identity provider is set to IAS. Conditional authentication is configured such that employee logging in with an email address ending in client domain (@xyz.com) are redirected to the SSO login page, while all other users are directed to the IAS login page where they authenticate using a username and password.
Real-time sync of onboardee is enabled in IAS

The client is not having any IP range for employee to configure in Conditional rule.

Current behaviour

All users(Employee and Onboardee) are redirecting to IAS login. Once they enter username or email address, employee redirect to SSO page and onboardee continue in IAS login page.

Client Requirement

When an employee accesses the SuccessFactors (SF) URL, they should be redirected directly to the SSO login page, bypassing the IAS login page. However, when an onboardee clicks the login link, they should be directed to the IAS login page for authentication.
Has anyone configured or designed a solution for a similar requirement?

Additionally, is the client's requirement feasible?

@jaideepshetty @zebashah @lukemarson @sushilgupta857 @abdulbasit 
SAP Single Sign-On SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Identity Provisioning #authentication

entry.title: Can Screens be customized in IAS?
entry.content: Specifically the User Profile Screen.

entry.title: Managed aspects in SAP CAPM stores the SCIM id of SAP CIS, not the email id of user
entry.content: We are using SAP CIS to manage the user authentication. When we are using managed aspect in the CDS entities, it stores the SCIM id instead of Email id, Can anyone help in overriding the behavior of managed aspect centrally in the MTA app
